report_results.executive_summary: InsightForge analyzed election.csv with 4205 rows and 10 columns. The workflow identified a regression task and selected RandomForestRegressor as the strongest model. The best score was 0.9814. Data quality scored 98.18/100 after automated cleaning.
business_insights: Data cleaning removed 52 rows and 1 columns.
RandomForestRegressor was selected on churn-aware validation: r2=0.9814, ahead of LinearRegression by +0.0874. Weighted F1=0.0000, macro F1=0.0000.
The top 3 most influential features were Total Votes, EVM Votes, and Postal Votes. Total Votes had a mean absolute SHAP value of 6.022, indicating it has the strongest impact on predictions.
'EVM Votes' and 'Total Votes' are strongly correlated (1.00).
key_findings: Data cleaning removed 52 rows and 1 columns.
RandomForestRegressor was selected on churn-aware validation: r2=0.9814, ahead of LinearRegression by +0.0874. Weighted F1=0.0000, macro F1=0.0000.
The top 3 most influential features were Total Votes, EVM Votes, and Postal Votes. Total Votes had a mean absolute SHAP value of 6.022, indicating it has the strongest impact on predictions.

recommendations: Prioritize churn-class recall, PR-AUC, and threshold tradeoffs over weighted F1 for business decisions.
Validate the selected model on fresh, unseen data before production deployment.
Monitor data drift and retrain when feature distributions or target balance change materially.
risk_analysis: Weighted metrics can hide minority-class churn errors.
Model quality must be revalidated after data drift or campaign policy changes.
report_results.deployment_readiness: Ready for offline stakeholder review; not production-ready until threshold policy, monitoring, and fresh holdout validation are completed.
model_limitations: Historical churn labels may encode past retention policies.
Correlation and SHAP explanations are directional diagnostics, not causal proof.
report_results.model_conclusion: RandomForestRegressor is recommended with R2=0.9814 on the holdout set.
